text: A three-centimeter incision is made in the right lower quadrant, excising a disc-shaped section of skin. The surgeon enters the abdomen through a muscle-splitting incision and establishes pneumoperitoneum using a single port gel point system. The small bowel is inspected, and the right colon is identified from the ileocolic pedicle, which is then divided.

The surgeon performs medial to lateral separation of the right colon in a cephalad direction, detaching lateral attachments. The hepatic flexure and terminal ileum are mobilized, and the omentum is dissected off the transverse colon. The middle colic vessels are divided, preserving the duodenum. The splenic flexure is taken down, extending dissection to the left colon.

The surgeon mobilizes the rectosigmoid colon and staples across the rectosigmoid junction in the distal sigmoid area, ensuring the region is well vascularized and positioned in the pelvis. The stapled end is grasped, and the entire specimen is exteriorized through the right lower quadrant stoma site. The area is narrowed using zero polysorb sutures, and the stoma is matured in place.
